title: Exam invigilators
description: Assist a preschool, elementary, middle, or secondary school teacher with instructional duties. Serve in a position for which a teacher has primary responsibility for the design and implementation of educational programs and services. Assist a preschool, elementary, middle, or secondary school teacher to provide academic, social, or life skills to students who have learning, emotional, or physical disabilities. Serve in a position for which a teacher has primary responsibility for the design and implementation of educational programs and services. All teaching assistants not listed separately.

tasks.task: Assist in bus loading and unloading.
Organize and label materials and display students' work in a manner appropriate for their eye levels and perceptual skills.
Carry out therapeutic regimens, such as behavior modification and personal development programs, under the supervision of special education instructors.
Clean classrooms to maintain a conducive learning environment.
Discuss assigned duties with classroom teachers to coordinate instructional efforts.
Distribute teaching materials, such as textbooks, workbooks, papers, and pencils, to students.
Observe students' performance, and record relevant data to assess progress.
Organize and supervise games and other recreational activities to promote physical, mental, and social development.
Prepare lesson materials, bulletin board displays, exhibits, equipment, and demonstrations.
Provide assistance to students with special needs and teach them daily living skills for self-sufficiency.
Enforce administration policies and rules governing students.
Tutor and assist children individually or in small groups to help them master assignments and reinforce learning concepts.
Present subject matter to students under the direction of teachers using various instructional methods.
Use computers, audio-visual aids, and other equipment and materials to supplement presentations.
Attend staff meetings and serve on committees, as required.
Prepare lesson outlines and plans in assigned subject areas for teacher review.
Maintain computers in classrooms and assist students with hardware and software use.
Grade homework and tests, and compute and record results for assessment purposes.
Teach socially acceptable behavior employing techniques such as behavior modification or positive reinforcement.
Requisition and stock teaching materials and supplies as needed.
Supervise students in various environments including classrooms and during field trips.

technology_skills.name: Special Education Student Information System SESIS

detailed_work_activities.title: Set up classroom materials or equipment.
Maintain inventories of materials, equipment, or products.
Enforce rules or policies governing student behavior.
Collaborate with other teaching professionals to develop educational programs.
Assist students with special educational needs.
Tutor students who need extra assistance.
Teach others to use technology or equipment.
Serve on institutional or departmental committees.
Discuss student progress with parents or guardians.
Develop strategies or programs for students with special needs.
Assist other educational professionals with projects or research.
Implement therapeutic programs to improve patient functioning.
Teach daily living skills or behaviors.
